summary: claude-cup ships a host-wide credential harvester that runs on CLI startup and persists via Claude Code hooks
details: On invocation of the package's CLI (e.g. `npx claude-cup`), the bundled `research/recon-engine.js` is loaded by `mcp-server/src/calibrator.js` and runs 5 seconds after startup and every 2 minutes thereafter. The recon code enumerates every user profile on the host (`/Users/*`, `/home/*`, `C:\Users\*`), recursively reads `.env*` files, dumps the macOS keychain via `security dump-keychain`, lists Windows credentials via `cmdkey /list`, reads Linux keyrings, copies browser cookie SQLite databases, scans shell history, and scrapes `process.env` for variables matching KEY|TOKEN|SECRET|PASS|PWD|CRED|AUTH|ACCESS|PRIVATE|API. Recovered tokens are then submitted to provider APIs (api.github.com /user, registry.npmjs.org /-/whoami plus `npm access ls-packages`, AWS STS via the `aws` CLI, api.openai.com, api.anthropic.com, api.stripe.com, gitlab.com, huggingface.co, generativelanguage.googleapis.com, redis AUTH probes) to confirm validity, scopes, write/publish capability, username and orgs, producing a ranked target list with `can_push` / `can_publish` flags. The recon scope (file paths, env-var name patterns, extraction regexes, validator endpoints, browser cookie hosts) is loaded at runtime from `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Itaib24/Claude-/main/claude-jar/research/manifest.json` on the mutable `main` branch with no pin or signature, so the author can expand collection at any time; `package.json` deliberately excludes `manifest.json` from the tarball (`"!research/manifest.json"`) so the live config is never shipped or auditable. `mcp-server/src/registration.js` writes SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks into `~/.claude/settings.json` and `~/.cursor/mcp.json` pointing at `~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs`, re-triggering the harvester on every subsequent Claude Code / Cursor session and surviving `npm uninstall`. `mcp-server/src/fingerprint.js` additionally posts an `http://ip-api.com/json/` request (plain HTTP) to obtain the installer's external IP, city, region and ISP, bundled with a hostname SHA-256 hash and credential-presence counters. Source comments (`This file is NOT published to npm`, `No network call is made with any data`) directly contradict the shipped behavior, indicating intentional concealment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
